title: The flex container
description: display: flex flips the layout mode.

questions.prompt: You add `display: flex` to a parent. What happens to its children?
options: They become flex items and lay out in a row by default
questions.explanation: Children of a flex container become flex items, arranged in a row by default.

questions.prompt: The default direction for a flex container is:

questions.explanation: display: flex turns .row into a flex container so gap works.
